buyer_segments.buyer_trigger: Enterprise customers ask whether hosted agents can retrieve context, call MCP tools, and delegate work without leaking data or inheriting unsafe authority.
buyer_segments.likely_objection: Needs evidence that the control layer works across model/provider boundaries, not just inside the public repo.
buyer_segments.proof_needed: Protocol conformance, context provenance, signed run receipts, prompt-injection regression, and customer-visible authorization decisions.
buyer_segments.title: Frontier Model Lab

buyer_segments.buyer_trigger: Needs a secure context and MCP governance layer that can be embedded into agent-builder, gateway, or workflow products.
buyer_segments.likely_objection: Needs productized auth, metering, private deployments, SLAs, and customer-specific policy mapping.
buyer_segments.proof_needed: Tenant isolation, private context registry, connector drift baselines, auth metadata checks, and trust-center export APIs.
buyer_segments.title: AI Platform Vendor

buyer_segments.buyer_trigger: Needs agentic AI and MCP security coverage to extend AppSec, identity, CNAPP, SOAR, or exposure-management portfolios.
buyer_segments.likely_objection: Needs live integrations with issue trackers, source hosts, SIEMs, identity providers, and connector inventories.
buyer_segments.proof_needed: Mappings to OWASP, NIST, MCP, identity, telemetry, incident response, and remediation workflow evidence.
buyer_segments.title: Security Platform Vendor

buyer_segments.buyer_trigger: Needs to approve agent pilots without granting broad human-equivalent authority or trusting unmanaged context.
buyer_segments.likely_objection: Needs private deployment, governance approval, procurement signoff, and evidence retention before production expansion.
buyer_segments.proof_needed: Private context ingestion, data-boundary controls, approval receipts, redacted telemetry, incident replay, and measurable reviewer-time reduction.
buyer_segments.title: Regulated Enterprise

diligence_questions.answer: The open corpus already proves breadth and category thinking, but acquisition-grade value requires customer telemetry, private context, hosted MCP controls, renewal signals, and evidence that security outcomes improve in real environments.
diligence_questions.question: Why is a design partner motion the next high-leverage step?

diligence_questions.answer: The pilot proves whether secure context, MCP authorization, tool-surface drift checks, run receipts, eval replay, and trust-center exports can reduce agent adoption friction while preserving security controls.
diligence_questions.question: What exactly does the design partner pilot prove?

diligence_questions.answer: The open project does not yet prove recurring revenue, multi-tenant hosted enforcement, customer-private evidence ingestion, operational SLAs, retention, expansion, or renewal value.
diligence_questions.question: What is not proven by the open project alone?

diligence_questions.answer: Public recipes, generated reference packs, local evaluators, standards mappings, docs, and the read-only MCP server should remain open so the project keeps credibility and adoption friction stays low.
diligence_questions.question: What should remain open to keep trust and distribution?

diligence_questions.answer: Hosted MCP policy, private secure-context registries, connector drift monitoring, run-receipt vaulting, trust-center APIs, customer-specific telemetry, and continuous eval replay are natural paid surfaces.
diligence_questions.question: What can be monetized without closing the corpus?

diligence_questions.answer: Start with one bounded remediation workflow, read-only or scoped branch-write access, private context hashing, MCP authorization decisions, receipts, verifier output, and reviewer-approved outcomes.
diligence_questions.question: What is the safest first design partner pilot?

diligence_questions.answer: Token passthrough, unregistered context retrieval, approval bypass, unsafe model routing, raw secret capture, connector capability expansion without review, or treating verifier failure as success should kill or pause the run.
diligence_questions.question: Which events should kill or pause the pilot immediately?

diligence_questions.answer: A buyer needs repeatable generated artifacts, deterministic decision points, customer telemetry, self-serve MCP access, evidence export, and a paid wedge that does not require bespoke manual work for every deployment.
diligence_questions.question: What convinces a buyer this is not servicesware?

diligence_questions.answer: An acquirer needs evidence of design partner pull, private context ingestion, hosted MCP enforcement, renewal value, category authority, and a control point that improves their enterprise agent trust story.
diligence_questions.question: What convinces an acquirer this can reach a $10-20M outcome?

diligence_questions.answer: Receipt completeness, context hash coverage, MCP decision coverage, reviewer-time reduction, automation success rate, safe hold behavior, incident replay completeness, and named expansion intent matter more than raw agent run volume.
diligence_questions.question: Which metrics matter most for renewal?

monetization_wedges.paid_surface: Protected MCP gateway, resource metadata checks, authorization conformance, tool-risk decisions, and signed policy receipts.
monetization_wedges.pricing_guardrail: Best suited for annual platform pricing once connected to live connector fleets.
monetization_wedges.proof_needed: Customer shows MCP calls are blocked, held, or allowed by policy before tool execution.
monetization_wedges.title: Hosted MCP policy plane

monetization_wedges.paid_surface: Tenant-side registry for customer context roots, source hashes, freshness checks, poisoning scans, evals, and recertification.
monetization_wedges.pricing_guardrail: Best suited for design-partner pilots where private evidence ingestion is the main blocker.
monetization_wedges.proof_needed: Customer-private context can be governed without exposing sensitive content to the public project.
monetization_wedges.title: Private secure context registry

monetization_wedges.paid_surface: Continuous discovery and diffing for MCP tool descriptions, schemas, annotations, capabilities, scopes, and server metadata.
monetization_wedges.pricing_guardrail: Best suited for security platforms and AI platform vendors with many MCP servers.
monetization_wedges.proof_needed: A design partner sees changed tool surfaces before agents trust them.
monetization_wedges.title: Connector drift monitoring

monetization_wedges.paid_surface: Retention, search, signing, export, and replay for agent run receipts, approvals, verifier output, telemetry, and incident evidence.
monetization_wedges.pricing_guardrail: Best suited for regulated enterprises and frontier labs selling agents to enterprise customers.
monetization_wedges.proof_needed: Customer can reconstruct why an agent was allowed, held, denied, or killed.
monetization_wedges.title: Run receipt vault

monetization_wedges.paid_surface: Customer-specific procurement, GRC, platform-intake, and acquisition diligence exports backed by live evidence hashes.
monetization_wedges.pricing_guardrail: Best suited for enterprise packaging and renewal evidence after the first pilot.
monetization_wedges.proof_needed: Buyer can answer diligence questions without bespoke spreadsheet work.
monetization_wedges.title: Trust-center API

monetization_wedges.paid_surface: Recurring prompt-injection, context-poisoning, authorization, egress, approval, and incident replay tests against customer workflows.
monetization_wedges.pricing_guardrail: Best suited for recurring subscription value and expansion into model/provider governance.
monetization_wedges.proof_needed: Customer sees regressions before a model, connector, prompt, or policy change reaches production.
monetization_wedges.title: Continuous agentic eval replay

hard_gates: No production write authority until approval receipts, entitlement review, and MCP authorization conformance are all linked to the run.
No raw customer logs, secrets, private keys, signing material, or unrestricted personal data can be exported into the open evidence layer.
Every retrieved context package must carry source ids, hashes, freshness state, and citation requirements.
Every pilot workflow must produce a run receipt, telemetry event, verifier result, and reviewer outcome before value is counted.
Any token passthrough, prompt-injection execution, unauthorized model route, or connector capability expansion kills the pilot run.
The design partner must be able to revoke agent identity, disable MCP scopes, and freeze evidence retention without vendor intervention.
Synthetic success metrics cannot be used as renewal proof unless customer runtime telemetry confirms the same pattern.

exit_criteria: Buyer segment and primary wedge are selected.
Workflow is bounded to read-only or scoped branch-write actions.
Pilot success metrics and telemetry fields are approved by security, platform, and legal stakeholders.
kill_or_hold_signals: Buyer requires unrestricted production write authority in phase one.
No accountable owner exists for identity revocation or evidence retention.
pilot_phases.objective: Select one bounded workflow, one agent host, one MCP connector class, success metrics, data boundaries, and revocation owners before any customer context is connected.
pilot_phases.title: Qualify and scope

exit_criteria: Private context registry is defined with owner and recertification cadence.
Context eval cases include poisoning, stale-source, and citation-failure paths.
kill_or_hold_signals: Private context cannot be hashed or owner-attested.
Telemetry would expose raw secrets, unrestricted customer logs, or personal data.
pilot_phases.objective: Register customer-private context roots, source owners, context hashes, freshness expectations, and prohibited data classes without exporting sensitive material into the open corpus.
pilot_phases.title: Bind private context

exit_criteria: At least ten controlled read-only runs produce complete receipts.
MCP authorization and tool-surface decisions are recorded for each run.
Eval failures are triaged with a hold, deny, or kill decision instead of being ignored.
kill_or_hold_signals: Observed MCP tool schema or description drifts without review.
pilot_phases.objective: Prove agents can retrieve governed context, cite hashes, pass evals, and produce run receipts before any write or approval-required MCP action is enabled.
pilot_phases.title: Run read-only MCP

exit_criteria: At least two workflow runs produce reviewer-approved outputs or structured triage notes.
Approval receipts match the action scope and expire correctly.
Incident replay can reconstruct context, identity, MCP decisions, and verifier output.
kill_or_hold_signals: Approval bypass, token passthrough, or model route mismatch appears in a run.
Verifier or reviewer failure is treated as success in value reporting.
pilot_phases.objective: Enable one scoped action class, such as branch-write remediation, only after approval receipts, entitlement review, verifier checks, and incident response evidence are wired into the run.
pilot_phases.title: Govern controlled actions

exit_criteria: Customer telemetry replaces default assumptions for at least one ROI scenario.
Renewal or expansion decision is tied to measured evidence completeness, reviewer-time reduction, and security outcome quality.
Paid wedges are ranked by buyer willingness to pay and implementation lift.
kill_or_hold_signals: Customer cannot share enough telemetry to prove value.
Pilot requires bespoke manual services that do not map to hosted MCP or secure-context product surfaces.
pilot_phases.objective: Replace assumption-based ROI with customer telemetry, renewal evidence, expansion triggers, and a board-ready package that states what is proven and what still remains unproven.
pilot_phases.title: Prove renewal case

positioning.buyer: AI platform leaders, product security, AppSec, security architecture, GRC, procurement security, model-risk, VC, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: Design partner pilot kit for secure context, MCP governance, and agentic AI security
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes makes AI easier to approve by packaging secure-context evidence, MCP control decisions, run receipts, eval replay, telemetry, and commercial proof gates into a pilot motion that a buyer can inspect before committing to production agent workflows.
positioning.why_now: NIST, OWASP, MCP, and CISA guidance has converged on the same practical problem: agent systems need constrained access, trusted context, protocol conformance, evidence, and measurable security outcomes before enterprises can scale them beyond pilots.

pricing_guardrails.annual_platform_acv_usd: 60000-180000 after hosted controls, private context, receipts, and connector monitoring are proven with at least one design partner.
pricing_guardrails.conversion_condition: Convert only when the customer can name the paid wedge, budget owner, renewal metric, and production control path.
pricing_guardrails.design_partner_pilot_usd: 15000-35000 for a 90-day private-context or MCP-governance pilot when customer telemetry and evidence export are included.
what_not_to_sell_yet: Live connector enforcement without tenant isolation
Revenue projections not backed by customer telemetry

risk_register.mitigation: Lead pilots with hosted MCP policy, private context registry, receipts, or trust-center API proof instead of generic content access.
risk_register.risk: Buyer sees the project as a documentation library rather than a product.

risk_register.mitigation: Limit phase one to one workflow, one agent host, one connector class, and explicit revocation ownership.
risk_register.risk: Pilot attempts too many workflows, connectors, or action classes at once.

risk_register.mitigation: Capture metadata, hashes, decisions, timings, and redacted outcomes by default; keep private content tenant-side.
risk_register.risk: Telemetry needed for ROI may expose customer secrets, logs, or personal data.

risk_register.mitigation: Label assumptions explicitly and require customer runtime telemetry before renewal or acquisition claims.
risk_register.risk: Synthetic demo metrics are mistaken for production value.

risk_register.mitigation: Bind pilots to source-backed profiles and rerun generator checks when standards or protocol references change.
risk_register.risk: MCP, agent identity, and agent security guidance changes faster than the pilot artifacts.

risk_register.mitigation: Keep the repo server read-only and explicitly sell production auth, metering, audit logging, and entitlement checks as hosted or deployment-layer controls.
risk_register.risk: Open reference MCP server is mistaken for production-authenticated hosted infrastructure.

risk_register.mitigation: Use connector intake and tool-surface drift evidence before changed tools are trusted by agents.
risk_register.risk: A vendor MCP server changes tool descriptions, schemas, annotations, or scopes during the pilot.

top_signals.title: Agent handoffs are becoming a protocol trust boundary
Indirect prompt injection is now social engineering against agents
MCP token audience, passthrough, and scope creep are control-plane risks
Agent identities are becoming a new non-human identity class
Capability-based risk scoring is becoming the enterprise gate for autonomous agents

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
source_references.why_it_matters: A serious pilot must prove MCP protected-resource metadata, resource indicators, token audience validation, PKCE, scope handling, and token-passthrough denial before production authority is granted.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol 2025-11-25 Key Changes
source_references.why_it_matters: Protocol drift, incremental scope consent, URL elicitation, task support, and metadata changes create design-partner value for continuous conformance and drift monitoring.

source_references.name: AI Agent Standards Initiative
source_references.why_it_matters: NIST is prioritizing interoperable agent protocols, agent authentication, identity infrastructure, and security evaluations, which validates a secure-context and MCP governance pilot.

source_references.name: CAISI Request for Information on Securing AI Agent Systems
source_references.why_it_matters: CAISI calls out indirect prompt injection, data poisoning, harmful agent actions, measurement, and deployment interventions that constrain and monitor agent access.

source_references.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
source_references.why_it_matters: OWASP gives design partners shared language for agent goal hijack, tool misuse, identity abuse, context poisoning, insecure inter-agent communication, cascading failures, and rogue-agent risk.

source_references.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
source_references.why_it_matters: The profile supports AI lifecycle governance, measurement, monitoring, data provenance, third-party risk, and incident response expectations that pilots should evidence.

source_references.name: Joint Guidance on Deploying AI Systems Securely
source_references.why_it_matters: Deployment guidance emphasizes confidentiality, integrity, availability, vulnerability mitigation, detection, and response for AI systems and related data and services.

source_references.name: Secure by Design
source_references.why_it_matters: CISA's secure-by-design principles push vendors to own customer security outcomes, be transparent, and make secure defaults part of the product rather than paid add-ons.

success_metrics.target: First governed run within 14 calendar days of kickoff
success_metrics.title: Time to first controlled run

success_metrics.target: >= 95% of counted runs include identity, context hash, authorization decision, verifier result, and reviewer outcome
success_metrics.title: Run receipt completeness

success_metrics.target: 100% of returned context packages include registered source ids and hashes
success_metrics.title: Context hash coverage

success_metrics.target: >= 95% of MCP tool calls have allow, hold, deny, or kill decisions before execution
success_metrics.title: MCP decision coverage

success_metrics.target: >= 25% reduction in reviewer minutes per accepted remediation output by week eight
success_metrics.title: Reviewer time saved

success_metrics.target: >= 50% of eligible pilot findings produce reviewer-approved PRs or structured triage notes
success_metrics.title: Automation success rate

success_metrics.target: 100% of ambiguous context, authorization, eval, or approval states hold instead of silently proceeding
success_metrics.title: Safe hold rate

success_metrics.target: >= 90% of sampled runs can reconstruct context, identity, tool decisions, approvals, and verifier output
success_metrics.title: Incident replay completeness

success_metrics.target: At least one customer-private context registry can be hashed, redacted, evaluated, and recertified without leaking data into the open corpus
success_metrics.title: Private context ingestion readiness

success_metrics.target: Design partner identifies a paid wedge, budget owner, and expansion trigger by the final readout
success_metrics.title: Renewal intent
